goal: Build always-on HTTP RAG server on port 52849 (Node stdlib http) wrapping CODEREF-CORE vector RAG (SemanticSearchService + OllamaProvider + SQLiteVectorStore) so cross-runtime callers (Python LLOYD, Node ASSISTANT, future surfaces) can query CORE RAG via HTTP without package import or per-request subprocess spawn. Local-only Ollama embeddings (per /feedback_coderef_core_rag_local). System-service posture: starts at boot/fast-start, lives independently, Phase 5 of WO-ROUTER-V1-001 depends on this.

phases.name: SCAFFOLD
phases.description: SCAFFOLD: src/cli/coderef-rag-server.ts using Node stdlib http on port 52849 (configurable via --port). --help prints usage. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Bin registered in package.json. build:cli emits dist file.

tasks.description: Author src/cli/coderef-rag-server.ts: stdlib http server on port 52849, --port and --help flags, request router stub, graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown.
Register coderef-rag-server in package.json bin map.
Build via npm run build:cli; verify dist/src/cli/coderef-rag-server.js exists.
Smoke test: coderef-rag-server --help prints usage, exits 0.
Commit Phase 1: SCAFFOLD HTTP server skeleton.

phases.name: HEALTH+STATUS
phases.description: HEALTH+STATUS: GET /api/health returns 200 {status:'healthy', version, uptime_s}. GET /api/rag/status returns Ollama-reachable bool, model name, embedding dimensions, sqlite-store info. Status endpoint MUST work even if Ollama is unreachable.

tasks.description: Implement GET /api/health -> 200 {status:'healthy', version, uptime_s, pid}.

Implement GET /api/rag/status -> probes Ollama (try fetch http://localhost:11434/api/tags within 2s timeout), reports ollama_reachable bool, model, dim, sqlite-store path. Always 200, never crashes on Ollama-down.
Spawn server in test mode (random port), curl /api/health and /api/rag/status, assert both 200.
Commit Phase 2: health + status endpoints.

phases.name: QUERY+INDEX
phases.description: QUERY+INDEX: POST /api/rag/query body {project_dir, query, top_k, lang?, type?} returns {results:[{path,score,chunk,metadata}], elapsed_ms, model}. POST /api/rag/index body {project_dir} runs idempotent re-index by spawning rag-index CLI as subprocess. Reuses SemanticSearchService.

tasks.description: Implement POST /api/rag/query: parse JSON body {project_dir, query, top_k, lang?, type?}; instantiate SemanticSearchService(OllamaProvider, SQLiteVectorStore[project_dir]); call .search(); return {results, elapsed_ms, model}. Versioned response header X-Coderef-RAG-API: 1.
Implement POST /api/rag/index: spawn rag-index CLI as subprocess against {project_dir}; idempotent (uses incremental-indexer); return {status, duration_ms, indexed_files, exit_code}.
Contract test: spawn server, POST /api/rag/query against current project, assert results array shape (each has path, score, metadata).
Commit Phase 3: query + index endpoints.

phases.name: DEGRADED-PATH
phases.description: DEGRADED-PATH: When Ollama is unreachable, /api/rag/query returns 503 {degraded:true, reason:'ollama_unreachable', detail}. Server does NOT crash. /api/health stays 200. /api/rag/status returns ollama_reachable:false. Add explicit health-probe before each query.

tasks.description: Pre-query Ollama health probe (cached 5s); on unreachable, return 503 {degraded:true, reason:'ollama_unreachable', detail, cached_at}. Server log warns, does NOT crash.
Same envelope for /api/rag/index when Ollama unreachable. /api/health and /api/rag/status remain 200.
Contract test for degraded path: point CODEREF_LLM_BASE_URL at unreachable port (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:65531), POST query, assert 503 + degraded:true + reason.
Commit Phase 4: degraded-path semantics.

phases.name: CONTRACT-DOCS
phases.description: CONTRACT-DOCS: docs/rag-http-api.md with curl examples for every endpoint, request/response JSON schemas, error envelope, degraded-mode contract. Versioned (X-Coderef-RAG-API: 1).

tasks.description: Author docs/rag-http-api.md: overview, endpoint table, per-endpoint request/response JSON contract + curl example, error envelope, degraded-mode contract, version header policy, port assignment.
Add rag-http-api.md to docs/CLI.md See Also section + a one-line overview row in the command table.
Commit Phase 5: contract docs.

phases.name: FAST-START+DEPLOY
phases.description: FAST-START+DEPLOY: Add coderef-rag-server slot to CODEREF-FAST-START.md (service start/stop/probe). docs/DEPLOY-CODEREF-RAG-SERVER.md with per-OS service-unit notes (systemd/launchd/Windows Service/pm2/manual) - reuses chokidar daemon's DEPLOY pattern.

tasks.description: Author docs/DEPLOY-CODEREF-RAG-SERVER.md: 5 service-unit patterns (systemd / launchd / Windows Service via NSSM / pm2 / manual). Reuse the structure of docs/DEPLOY-CODEREF-WATCH.md so the docs feel consistent.
Add coderef-rag-server slot to CODEREF-FAST-START.md (or coderef-core-quickstart.md if FAST-START doesn't yet exist) - start command, expected port, health probe.
Verify always-on posture and consumer-callable contract are explicitly stated in DEPLOY doc.
Commit Phase 6: fast-start + deployment story.

phases.name: CLOSE
phases.description: CLOSE: standard 5-step close (Phase 6 universal gate fires; smoke-test from curl; final commits + push).

tasks.description: Run /close-workorder against this WO (manual close path).
Verify Phase 6 universal gate fired and regenerated foundation-docs.
Run validate-docs.js to confirm freshness gate exits 0.
Commit Phase 7: WO-RAG-HTTP-SERVER-V1-001 close.
Hard stop: final approval before TRACKING marks completed.

plan_type_rationale: Multi-phase, multi-concern server build: SCAFFOLD (HTTP entrypoint + bin) -> HEALTH+STATUS endpoints -> QUERY+INDEX endpoints (RAG integration) -> DEGRADED-PATH (Ollama-down semantics) -> CONTRACT-DOCS (rag-http-api.md) -> FAST-START+DEPLOY (CODEREF-FAST-START.md slot + per-OS notes) -> CLOSE. Each phase has its own gate (build, smoke, contract test, doc check). Multi-runtime concern (Python LLOYD will consume HTTP from Node server) requires explicit contract phase. Phase 5 of WO-ROUTER-V1-001 depends on this WO shipping; phased separation lets ORCHESTRATOR observe gate boundaries cleanly.
